mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a walk-forward t-test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate earnings revisions and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks and manufacturing contraction, which suppresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods following an industrial production decline, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 5.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 5.0% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and forces immediate negative S&P 500 repricing over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect disappears when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, which systematically generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters following positive GDP growth, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising inflation indicates stagflationary pressures that erode profit margins and trigger risk-off sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the joint condition fails to predict return direction with a p-value below 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.
